channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Bipartisan Senate bill targets money laundering linked to drug trafficking, terrorism
item.description: Senate Judiciary Committee Chairman Chuck Grassley and Sen. Amy Klobuchar introduced a bipartisan measure to crack down on money laundering by increas...

item.title: Local leaders and lawmen defy Democrat-run states' crackdown on cooperating with ICE
item.description: As Democratic politicians in blue states rush to shut down cooperation with ICE, a rebellion is brewing among conservative local politicians who say t...

item.title: The Super Bowl Halftime Show Was Literally About Erasing ‘America’
item.description: Bad Bunny naming the entire western hemisphere as one America was an open endorsement of open borders and the implicit rejection of nationhood.

item.title: The man who fixed Walmart’s grocery business was just appointed CEO of Kroger
item.description: Former Walmart U.S. chief Greg Foran is just the direct, no-nonsense CEO the supermarket chain urgently needs.

item.title: Epstein victims use Super Bowl commercial to pressure Pam Bondi over withheld files
item.description: Super Bowl commercial targets Pam Bondi over Jeffrey Epstein files, with women demanding truth from attorney general. DOJ disputes claims it failed tr...

item.title: The Trump Administration Defeated The ‘Seditious Six’ By ‘Going On Offense’
item.description: The 'Seditious Six' called for a mutiny, and the administration decided to 'go on offense and make them tap-dance a little.'

item.title: How Sheikh Tahnoon Bought the UAE’s Way into Trump World
item.description: When Trump won the 2024 election, Tahnoon saw his opportunity to influence the U.S. government.

item.title: A Hong Kong Tragedy — and a Test for the U.S.
item.description: In the wake of Jimmy Lai’s sentencing, the U.S. must stand up for democratic values.

item.title: Ford worker who heckled Trump won't be disciplined or lose job
item.description: The autoworker who heckled President Trump during his visit to a Michigan plant last month will keep his job and won't be disciplined, his union annou...

item.title: Jennifer Garner’s Once Upon a Farm IPO jumps 40% as the company raises $198 million
item.description: Once Upon a Farm started trading on the New York Stock Exchange Friday.

item.title: The Best Chagos Deal Is No Deal
item.description: The Trump administration should step up political pressure on Starmer to change course.

item.title: Rubio blasts China over 'unjust and tragic' 20-year sentence for Hong Kong democracy activist Jimmy Lai
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio calls Jimmy Lai's 20-year sentence "unjust" as Hong Kong convicts the 78-year-old democracy activist under national sec...

item.title: Pro-China Forces Sentence Hong Kong Freedom Fighter Jimmy Lai To 20 Years In Prison
item.description: In a further erosion of freedom in Hong Kong, the city’s Chinese-backed government sentenced media mogul and pro-democracy activist Jimmy Lai to 20 ye...

item.title: Hagerty urges FCC to punish Verizon over release of Senate phone data
item.description: Verizon handed over phone data from Senator Bill Hagerty and other GOP senators to Jack Smith's team investigating President Donald Trump and 2020 ele...

item.title: Mike Macdonald’s Christian Worship Was The Bright Spot In This Year’s Depraved Super Bowl
item.description: It goes without saying that this year’s Super Bowl was an absolute disaster. From the morally depraved and anti-American halftime show to the terrible...

item.title: The Trump family’s crypto portfolio is getting battered with the rest of the industry—but Melania’s memecoin has fared surprisingly well
item.description: A recent collapse in the price of Bitcoin has spurred weakness across the sector.

item.title: Judge orders feds to pay to bring back deported illegal immigrants
item.description: Taxpayers suddenly find themselves on the hook for bringing back illegal immigrants that judges say were wrongly deported.

item.title: Scott Galloway on why that Anthropic Super Bowl ad got under Sam Altman’s skin and exposed ‘therapy’ as the AI use case
item.description: The marketing guru and host of Prof G Markets said the ad was a “seminal moment” in the AI wars and will lead to Anthropic being more valuable than Op...

item.title: DOD employee moonlighted as money mule for Nigerian fraudsters: Feds
item.description: Federal authorities announced charges Monday against a Defense Department employee they say was secretly working as a money mule for a Nigerian fraud ...

item.title: ‘Every fight I have ever been in, I’ve won’: British PM Starmer vows to fight for his job after Epstein links sack cabinet
item.description: Starmer apologized to Epstein's victims last week and said he was sorry for “having believed Mandelson’s lies.”

item.title: Turning Point issues major endorsement in critical Senate race as Trump hints at weighing in
item.description: Turning Point Action has endorsed Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton in his bid to unseat longtime incumbent Sen. John Cornyn, R-Texas, in a high-stake...

item.title: Left-wing upset brewing in New Jersey after pro-Israel push against establishment Democrat
item.description: The left wing of the Democratic Party is on the verge of a win in the race to replace Gov. Mikie Sherrill in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District,...

item.title: Tony Hinchcliffe Was Right
item.description: Despite Bad Bunny's arrogance, Puerto Rico would probably more resemble a hellscape than anything else without the patronage of the American people.

item.title: Rethinking Household Asset Allocation Under Capital Constraints
item.description: Leveraged ETFs aren’t about more risk, they’re about using less capital to achieve a desired risk profile in household portfolios. The post Rethinking...

item.title: Goodbye, Kostyantynivka
item.description: Not too long ago, the eastern Ukrainian city helped build my family. Now, the town is little more than ruins.

item.title: Thirty Candles for the Internet’s Foundation
item.description: Internet free speech is celebrating its 30th birthday — if Section 230’s opponents don’t spoil the party, that is.

item.title: Stockholm’s Capital Markets Success: More Than Meatballs
item.description: Stockholm’s capital-market success reflects more than IPOs and PE activity. It offers a case study in how culture, institutions, and incentives shape ...

item.title: Where AI Ends and Investment Judgment Begins
item.description: AI’s growing capabilities challenge traditional investment skill, shifting competitive advantage toward governance, process, and judgment. The post Wh...

item.title: Three Risks of Relying on the S&P 500 in Retirement Planning
item.description: Why S&P 500 reliance can undermine retirement outcomes, and how diversification, valuation discipline, and withdrawals reshape long-term portfolio ris...

item.title: Book Review: Principles of Bitcoin
item.description: A rigorous exploration of bitcoin reveals a new framework for understanding money beyond traditional financial models. The post Book Review: Principle...
